title: JavaScript Placement Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on where and how to place JavaScript in HTML documents.

questions.question: Which HTML tag is used to include JavaScript in an HTML file?
What happens if you place a script in the <head> of an HTML document without async or defer?
options: The browser pauses HTML parsing to download and execute the script.

questions.question: What is the recommended position for a <script> tag to avoid delaying the initial rendering of a page?
options: At the very top of the <head>.

questions.question: What does the 'defer' attribute do for external script tags?
options: It delays the download until a user clicks a button.
It downloads the script asynchronously but executes it only after the HTML is fully parsed.
It prevents the script from running entirely.
It only works for scripts that are smaller than 10KB.

questions.question: True or False: Using 'async' guarantees that scripts will execute in the order they appear in the HTML.
questions.comment: Scripts with the async attribute are executed as soon as they are downloaded, regardless of their order.
